response: The Legal/Engineering team shared results of a completed open source software dependency audit, which scanned 234 total packages and identified two problematic licensing issues that conflict with the company’s SaaS model: a charting library relying on the incompatible AGPL “viral” license and a second package with no license at all. The team recommends resolving both issues ahead of the company’s next release by swapping the AGPL-licensed charting library for the safe, Apache-licensed Chart.js alternative and removing the unlicensed package from the project’s dependencies entirely.
